So, the algorithm is:

Initialize left=0, right = s.length()-1.

total_removed =0.

while (left < right) {

   if (s[left] != s[right]) break;

   char c = s[left];

   // find max prefix length.

   int max_l = 0;

   while (left + max_l < s.length() && s[left + max_l] == c) max_l++;

   // find max suffix length.

   int max_r =0;

   while (right - max_r >=0 && s[right - max_r] == c) max_r++;

   // the maximum we can take is such that max_l + max_r <= (right - left +1).

   // because the current string is from left to right, length is right - left +1.

   // So, the maximum possible is to take as much as possible without overlapping.

   // So, the maximum possible is min(max_l, max_r, (right - left +1) / 2).

   // Wait, no. Because the prefix can be any length up to max_l, and the suffix any up to max_r, as long as their sum is <= (right - left +1).

   // To maximize the total_removed, perhaps we should take as much as possible in each step.

   // So, take the maximum possible l and r such that l + r <= (right - left +1).

   // But l can be up to max_l, r up to max_r.

   // So, the maximum possible is l = max_l, r = max_r, but if l + r > current_length, then we have to reduce.

   current_length = right - left +1;

   possible_l = max_l;

   possible_r = max_r;

   if (possible_l + possible_r > current_length) {

      // can't take all, so take as much as possible.

      // the maximum possible is current_length -1, since l and r must be at least 1.

      int total = possible_l + possible_r;

      if (total > current_length) {

         // we can take l = current_length - possible_r, but that may not be possible.

         // Alternatively, take l = possible_l, r = min(possible_r, current_length - possible_l).

         r = min(possible_r, current_length - possible_l);

         l = possible_l;

      } else {

         l = possible_l;

         r = possible_r;

      }

   } else {

      l = possible_l;

      r = possible_r;

   }

   // but wait, l and r must be at least 1.

   if (l <1 || r <1) break;

   total_removed += l + r;

   left += l;

   right -= r;

}

So, the algorithm is:

Initialize left=0, right = s.length()-1.

total_removed =0.

while (left < right) {

   if (s[left] != s[right]) break;

   char c = s[left];

   // find maximum prefix length.

   int max_l =0;

   while (left + max_l < s.length() && s[left + max_l] == c) max_l++;

   // find maximum suffix length.

   int max_r =0;

   while (right - max_r >=0 && s[right - max_r] == c) max_r++;

   // the maximum possible l and r such that l + r <= (right - left +1).

   int current_length = right - left +1;

   int l = max_l;

   int r = max_r;

   if (l + r > current_length) {

      // can't take all, so take as much as possible.

      // the maximum possible is current_length -1, since l and r must be at least 1.

      // So, the maximum possible is l = current_length - r, but r can't exceed max_r.

      // Or, perhaps take l as much as possible, then r as much as possible.

      // Alternatively, take l = min(max_l, current_length -1 - r).

      // This is getting complicated.

      // Perhaps, in this case, the maximum possible is to take l = max_l, and r = min(max_r, current_length - l).

      r = min(max_r, current_length - l);

   }

   if (l >=1 && r >=1) {

      total_removed += l + r;

      left += l;

      right -= r;

   } else {

      break;

   }

}

So, the minimal length is s.length() - total_removed.
